name: Advance Payday Loans
description_short: Moneytree is a payday and installment loan lender offering short-term loans up to $700 with same-day funding options at a Kent, WA location.
description_long:
Moneytree operates as a licensed consumer finance lender providing emergency short-term loans through physical branch locations and online channels. The company is headquartered under MoneyTree Inc. and operates multiple branches across Washington state, with the East Hill location in Kent serving the local community since at least the time of this profile. Moneytree specializes in rapid-access small-dollar lending designed for consumers facing immediate cash needs before their next paycheck.

Moneytree offers three primary loan products: payday loans (short-term, single-payment loans up to $700), installment loans (repayable over multiple payments, also up to $700), and signature loans. Payday loans charge a flat fee structure of $15 per $100 borrowed up to $500, and $10 per $100 on amounts exceeding $500—resulting in a 391.07% APR on a $100 14-day loan. Installment loans provide longer repayment terms with biweekly payment options over periods like 70 days. Funding is available in cash, via ACH deposit to checking accounts (online only), or through Instant Funding to qualified debit cards in approximately 30-45 minutes. The Kent branch also offers check cashing, business check cashing, prepaid cards, and wire transfers.

Moneytree distinguishes itself through multiple funding delivery methods, including rapid debit card deposits via Instant Funding, and maintains relatively accessible application requirements (valid ID, Social Security Number, proof of income, and checking account). The company explicitly excludes military "covered borrowers" under the Military Lending Act and checks state borrowing databases to prevent over-extension. Extended business hours (until 7 PM on Fridays) accommodate working consumers, and the company provides transparent fee examples and APR disclosures upfront.

However, Moneytree's loan products carry substantial costs typical of payday lending. The 391% APR on payday loans far exceeds traditional credit products and federal payday-alternative benchmarks of 36% APR. The company's own customer notice warns that "small-dollar loans used over a long period of time can be expensive," acknowledging the debt trap risk. Installment loans, while offering longer terms, still carry significant fees. These products are best suited as genuine emergency solutions for single urgent expenses, not ongoing credit access.
pros: Multiple funding delivery options: cash, ACH transfer, and Instant Funding to debit cards (30-45 minutes)
Transparent flat-fee structure disclosed upfront ($15/$100 up to $500; $10/$100 over $500)
Extended branch hours until 7 PM on Fridays accommodates working consumers
Installment loan option provides longer repayment terms beyond standard 14-day payday cycles
Explicitly excludes military borrowers and checks state lending database to prevent over-borrowing
Additional services available at branch: check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, prepaid cards
Simple application requirements: valid ID, Social Security Number, proof of income, checking account
cons: Payday loans carry extremely high 391.07% APR, far exceeding 36% federal payday-alternative benchmarks
Flat-fee structure incentivizes rolling over loans, creating debt trap cycles despite company warnings
Maximum loan amount of $700 insufficient for most genuine emergencies (medical, car repair, housing)
Instant Funding dependent on debit card type and bank processing; may take longer than advertised 30-45 minutes
Limited repayment flexibility; short-term loans provide no grace period or hardship provisions for missed payments
best_for: Consumers with approved checking accounts needing immediate cash before payday for small specific expenses
Individuals without credit cards or traditional credit access who can repay the full amount in 14 days or less
Workers with irregular income who need bridge funding between paychecks and prefer in-person transactions
diagnosis: Moneytree is best for employed consumers with checking accounts facing genuine single-payment emergencies under $700 who can repay within 14 days. The critical caveat is that 391% APR payday loans should never be used for ongoing expenses or rolled over repeatedly—the company's own disclosure warns that small-dollar loans become expensive over time, a risk inherent to payday lending products regardless of lender quality.
services: Payday loans up to $700 with 14-day repayment terms
Installment loans up to $700 with extended repayment schedules (70+ days)
Instant Funding to qualified debit cards (30-45 minutes)
ACH bank account transfers (online loans only)
